dataset.title: NEON Biorepository Benthic Macroinvertebrate Collection (Taxonomy Reference Collection)

abstract.para:
Individual macroinvertebrate specimens retained as taxonomic reference material from samples collected from wadeable streams, rivers, and lakes. Collected samples are separated into fluid-preserved macroinvertebrates, fluid-preserved chironomids, fluid-preserved oligochaetes, and slide-mounted chironomids and oligochaetes. Taxonomic identifications are generated from subsamples of each group. This sample type contains macroinvertebrate specimens retained as taxonomic reference material from subsample analysis. Sampling
3 sampling bouts per year (spring, summer, fall) 8 locations at each NEON site
5 in dominant habitat type (wadeable stream) or littoral area (lake and river) 3 in sub-dominant habitat type (wadeable streams) or pelagic area (lakes and rivers)
11 samples collected per bout total
1 sample per location for morphological taxonomy (8 samples per bout) 1 sample at 3 of the dominant habitat type (wadeable stream) or littoral area (lake and river) locations for DNA metabarcoding (3 samples per bout)
Methods
Habitat-appropriate sampling gear (e.g., Surber, Hess, corer, kicknet, D-frame sweep, petite ponar) Samples collected from natural substrate surface
Preservation
Samples shipped to taxonomist for sorting and identification into general macroinvertebrates, chironomids, and oligochaetes
Taxonomic reference samples can be prepared and archived at the end of a taxonomist's contract period
Preserved in 95% ethanol Small volume of glycerol added prior to October 2021 Maintained in temperature- and humidity-controlled storage

intellectualRights.para: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution (CC-BY) 4.0 License.

contact.organizationName: NEON Biorepository Benthic Macroinvertebrate Collection (Taxonomy Reference Collection)

gbif.citation: National Ecological Observatory Network (2026). NEON Biorepository Benthic Macroinvertebrate Collection (Taxonomy Reference Collection). Occurrence dataset https://doi.org/10.15468/rrr784 accessed via GBIF.org on 2026-05-24.
